summary: Iteration 77 - Frontend-only testing for State Appeal Stats widget and iOS PDF export code review. All 13 test features verified successfully.
context_for_next_testing_agent: State Appeal Stats widget fully functional. All 8 state buttons (NSW/VIC/QLD/SA/WA/TAS/NT/ACT) work correctly. Each state panel shows: Appeals Filed, Allowed/Varied, Dismissed/Refused, Pending numbers; Conviction vs Sentence visual bar; 6 ranked appeal grounds with percentages; Crime types with percentage bars; Success rate badge; Source citation; Avg processing time. iOS PDF/DOCX export uses navigator.share() API with fallback in both ReportView.jsx and BarristerView.jsx. Landing page stats unchanged (8,700+, ~40%, 98%, 1 in 80). Report tier word counts unchanged (1,500-2,200 / 4,500-6,500 / 7,000-10,000).

state_appeal_stats_widget.section_positioning: PASSED (between dark section and See It In Action)

ios_export_code_pattern_verified.description: Both ReportView.jsx and BarristerView.jsx implement iosShareOrDownload() function that: 1) Detects iOS via /iPad|iPhone|iPod/.test(navigator.userAgent), 2) Uses navigator.share() API with File object if available, 3) Checks navigator.canShare() before sharing, 4) Falls back to window.location.href for iOS if share fails, 5) Uses standard download link for non-iOS devices
ios_export_code_pattern_verified.note: Cannot test actual iOS behavior from this environment - code pattern verified only
